methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $47M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Pivotal is a Growth-stage transportation company founded by Marcus Leng and backed by Page Capital. The relevant comp universe comprises three private frontier-transport peers (Rivian, The Boring Company, Einride); no public comparables exist in the narrow same-stage cohort.

Valuation analysis relies on a single applicable method: Stage-Progression. Using Pivotal's $47M total capital raised as a base proxy, application of a typical 1.2x step-up multiplier from Growth stage to the next inflection point yields an implied value of $56M. The Peer-Median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage Transportation peers meeting minimum sample thresholds (fewer than three datapoints), making any sector-wide median unreliable.

The triangulated range spans $48M (low) to $64M (high), with a median of $56M. Given reliance on a single method and the sparsity of direct comparables, this range reflects stage-appropriate step-up assumptions rather than market-validated peer multiples. Key uncertainties include the absence of recent funding rounds to validate momentum, limited peer depth in the narrow frontier-transport segment, and stage-specific multiplier volatility. Any valuation within the $48M–$64M band is defensible; precision beyond the range midpoint ($56M) is not supported by available data.
